title: Sythesis ignores ambiguous contruct without error or warning.
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

[bug01.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/5465238/bug01.zip)

```
ghdl bug01.vhd
yosys -s bug01.s
```

## Expected behavior

The ambiguous code is:
```
  process (i70,i85,i58,i84,i53,i75)
  begin
    if (i85(1) = '1') then
      v_i82_i79_i80_i81 <= i70;
    elsif (i84(0) = '1') then
      v_i82_i79_i80_i81 <= i58;
    elsif (i84(1) = '1') then
      v_i82_i79_i80_i81 <= i53;
    elsif (i85(0) = '1') then
      v_i82_i79_i80_i81 <= i75;
    end if;
  end process;
```

The cases are actually exhaustive within the context of the design but not provable without knowledge about i84/i85.

I would expect the tools to either:
1. treat the code as exhaustive and implement (other synthesis tools do this)
2. report an error

## Actual behavior

The logic is complete discarded (outputs xxxx) and no error or warning is given.
